title_zh: 消失的崩溃：五模型经济中的控制与涌现
title_en: The Crash That Vanished: Control and Emergence in a Five-Model Economy
summary_zh: 本文探讨了一个包含五个模型的经济体系中，如何观察到崩溃的消失现象，重点分析了控制变量与系统涌现行为之间的关系。
summary_en: This article explores a five-model economy where a crash seemingly vanishes, focusing on the interplay between control parameters and emergent system behaviors.
why_it_matters_zh: 理解复杂经济系统中的控制与涌现关系有助于更好地预测和管理潜在经济崩溃风险。
why_it_matters_en: Understanding the relationship between control and emergence in complex economic systems aids in better predicting and managing potential economic crashes.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: The Crash That Vanished: Control and Emergence in a Five-Model Economy. Category: tools_apps. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 通过将公平性视为对称操作来检测和缓解偏见
title_en: Detecting and Mitigating Bias by Treating Fairness as a Symmetry Operation
summary_zh: 在高风险社会经济环境中部署的机器学习系统经常表现出偏见。该论文将偏见形式化为一个对称破坏操作：如果在保持优点特征不变的情况下交换敏感属性后，分类器的输出保持不变，则该分类器被视为公平。通过基于损失的正则化作为对称恢复机制，在四个具有不同噪声、相关性和偏见水平的合成数据集上进行评估，该框架实现了超过90%的违规减少，准确率损失约为5%。该方法不依赖因果图知识，计算效率高，且适用于任何可定义为比特翻转的敏感属性，适合缺乏本地歧视来源的场景。
summary_en: Machine learning systems deployed in high-stakes socioeconomic settings often exhibit bias. This paper formalizes bias as a symmetry-breaking operation: a classifier is fair if its outputs remain invariant when switching a sensitive attribute while keeping merit features fixed. Using loss-based regularization as a symmetry restoring mechanism, the framework was evaluated on four synthetic datasets with varying noise, correlation, and bias levels, achieving over 90% violation reduction with about 5% accuracy cost. It requires no causal graph knowledge, is computationally lightweight, and generalizes to any sensitive attribute definable as a bit-flip, making it suitable where local discrimination sources are absent.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究提供了一种无需因果图知识、计算轻量且通用的公平性检测和缓解方法，适合现实中缺乏明确定义歧视来源的复杂环境，推动了机器学习公平性的理论与实践发展。
why_it_matters_en: This research offers a computationally efficient, general approach to detect and mitigate bias without requiring causal knowledge, suitable for complex real-world scenarios lacking clear discrimination sources, advancing both theory and practice in machine learning fairness.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Detecting and Mitigating Bias by Treating Fairness as a Symmetry Operation. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: DiBS：基于扩散模型的分支选择
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种名为DiBS的新方法，利用扩散模型指导数独问题的分支选择搜索过程，结合符号求解的完整性与扩散模型的全局引导，显著减少搜索成本，特别是在长尾搜索中表现出优势。
summary_en: This paper proposes DiBS, a novel approach leveraging diffusion models to guide branch selection in Sudoku solving, maintaining symbolic solver completeness while providing global guidance to reduce search costs, especially in challenging long-tail cases.
why_it_matters_zh: DiBS结合了扩散模型的全局结构学习能力与符号求解的严格保证，克服了传统深度学习解法缺乏正确性保证及符号解法搜索时间长的问题，在复杂实例上有效降低了搜索成本。
why_it_matters_en: DiBS integrates the global structural learning of diffusion models with the completeness of symbolic solvers, overcoming limitations of existing deep learning and symbolic methods by effectively reducing search costs in hard Sudoku instances.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: DiBS: Diffusion-Informed Branch Selection. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: SafeGene：可复用的安全适配器，实现安全对齐的可迁移性
title_en: SafeGene: Reusable Adapters for Transferable Safety Alignment
summary_zh: 本文提出了SafeGene，一种可复用的安全适配器模块，针对开源大语言模型（LLM）在进行下游微调时安全对齐性减弱导致的安全恢复问题。SafeGene 把安全能力作为独立且可复用的适配器表示，从对齐与降级模型差异中获取安全向量，经过数据感知层选择优化，并通过少样本层级系数重新校准用于各个下游任务中。多模型、多任务实验证明，SafeGene有效减少有害回答率，同时保持任务性能，优于现有安全适配方法。
summary_en: The paper introduces SafeGene, a reusable safety-adapter module designed to address the recurring safety alignment degradation in open-weight large language models during downstream fine-tuning. SafeGene treats safety capability as an independent, reusable adapter derived from discrepancies between aligned and degraded models, refined via data-aware layer selection, and applied through few-shot layer-wise coefficient recalibration in downstream tasks. Experiments across multiple model families and tasks show that SafeGene reduces harmful responses while maintaining performance, outperforming existing safe adaptation methods.
why_it_matters_zh: 由于微调可能削弱大模型的安全对齐，SafeGene通过解耦安全模块并复用适配器，有效提升模型在多任务环境中的安全表现，减少有害输出，推动了人工智能安全技术的进步。
why_it_matters_en: Because fine-tuning can weaken LLMs' safety alignment, SafeGene effectively enhances model safety across tasks by decoupling and reusing a dedicated safety adapter, reducing harmful outputs and advancing AI safety methodologies.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: SafeGene: Reusable Adapters for Transferable Safety Alignment. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 通过一致性驱动的强化学习提升跨语言事实回忆能力
title_en: Improving Cross-Lingual Factual Recall via Consistency-Driven Reinforcement Learning
summary_zh: 本论文介绍了PolyFact，一个包含12种语言、10万条基于Wikidata事实的多语种平行问答数据集，旨在研究大型语言模型在非英语语言中的事实表达不一致问题。研究比较了轻量持续预训练、监督微调和基于Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)的强化学习方法，发现在Qwen-2.5-7B和OLMo-2-1124-7B模型上，GRPO在跨语言一致性和对未见语言的泛化能力上均优于其他方法。此外，机制分析表明GRPO通过减弱多层感知机和注意力头中的语言特化，提高了共享的跨语言表示。研究团队将公开代码、模型和数据集。
summary_en: This work presents PolyFact, a large-scale parallel multilingual factual QA dataset with 100K Wikidata-based facts across 12 diverse languages, addressing the problem of cross-lingual factual inconsistency in large language models predominantly trained on English data. The study compares lightweight continual pretraining, supervised fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) on Qwen-2.5-7B and OLMo-2-1124-7B, finding that GRPO consistently outperforms with better cross-lingual consistency and generalization to unseen languages. Mechanistic analysis reveals that GRPO reduces language specialization in MLP layers and attention heads, fostering more shared cross-lingual representations. Code, models, and dataset are publicly released.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究为解决大型语言模型在多语言环境下事实表达不准确的问题提供了有效方法，尤其是通过强化学习技术提升了模型的跨语言一致性和泛化能力，对于推动多语言人工智能应用具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: This research provides an effective approach to address large language models' factual inconsistency across languages, notably enhancing cross-lingual consistency and generalization via reinforcement learning. It has significant implications for advancing multilingual AI applications.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Improving Cross-Lingual Factual Recall via Consistency-Driven Reinforcement Learning. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: UnpredictaBench：评估大型语言模型分布随机性的新基准
title_en: UnpredictaBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Distributional Randomness in LLMs
summary_zh: UnpredictaBench是一项测试大型语言模型（LLM）捕捉真实底层分布能力的评估基准。随着LLM越来越多地被用来替代其他实体（如经济模拟中的人类），模型趋向于仅给出单一合理答案，未能体现真实系统的不可预测性。UnpredictaBench设计了448个简化但核心的采样问题，涉及统计分布、随机程序和自然语言描述的随机过程。该基准引入KS@N评价指标，通过Kolmogorov-Smirnov检验量化模型样本与目标分布的接近度。测试显示各模型在分布采样能力上差异显著，最高也未超出40%，表明该能力仍有很大提升空间。即使引入推理，提升有限，表明简单的分布模拟对LLM依然是挑战，是将LLM用作复杂系统代理的必要前提。
summary_en: UnpredictaBench is an evaluation benchmark testing large language models' (LLMs) ability to capture true underlying distributions. As LLMs increasingly substitute other agents (e.g., humans in economic simulations), many models tend to collapse to a single plausible answer, failing to reflect real system unpredictability. UnpredictaBench introduces 448 simplified but fundamental sampling problems from canonical statistical distributions, stochastic programs, and natural language scenarios describing random processes. It proposes KS@N, a metric based on the Kolmogorov-Smirnov test, measuring how well model samples match target distributions. Tests on open and proprietary models reveal large variation and no model exceeding 40% success at KS@100, indicating major room for improvement. Adding reasoning slightly helps but is not a full solution. This benchmark highlights that even simple distributional simulation remains challenging, a crucial step toward employing LLMs as stand-ins for complex systems.
why_it_matters_zh: 随着LLM在模拟和替代真实系统代理中的应用增加，模型能否真实反映结果分布的随机特性变得至关重要。UnpredictaBench通过严谨的统计指标和丰富的问题库，揭示当前模型在随机采样方面的不足，强调了这一能力在实际应用中的必要性和挑战性。
why_it_matters_en: As LLMs are increasingly used to simulate or replace real system agents, the ability to accurately reflect outcome distributions' randomness is critical. UnpredictaBench, with its strict statistical metric and extensive problem set, exposes current models' shortcomings in randomized sampling, highlighting the necessity and difficulty of this capability for practical applications.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: UnpredictaBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Distributional Randomness in LLMs. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 泛化的搭便车假说：解释与缓解新兴错配问题
title_en: The Piggyback Hypothesis of Generalization: Explaining and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment
summary_zh: 本文针对大型语言模型（LLMs）在训练数据之外广泛过度泛化的问题，提出了搭便车假说：聊天模板的特定前缀标记可以将微调后的行为“搭便车”到域外查询。通过对前缀的细微扰动或用未微调模型的前缀表示替换，能在不改动用户查询的情况下恢复模型对齐。基于此，提出了Token正则化微调（TReFT）方法，在多模型和数据集上有效降低了新兴错配（EM），同时保留了领域内学习效果。TReFT在法律领域微调的Llama-3.1-8B模型上，相较于数据交叉训练减少了33.5%的EM，且在其他狭窄微调场景如回避、工具使用中同样表现优异（场外泛化平均减少54.3%）。研究揭示了LLMs可能以意外方式学习和泛化，并为更受约束的微调提供了新途径。
summary_en: This work addresses the broad over-generalization of large language models (LLMs) beyond training examples by proposing the Piggyback Hypothesis: chat-template prefix tokens can carry finetuned behavior onto out-of-domain queries. The hypothesis is validated by showing that subtle perturbations or patching of the prefix representations can restore alignment without changing user queries. Building on this, Token-Regularized Finetuning (TReFT) regularizes specific token representations during training to mitigate emergent misalignment (EM). TReFT reduces EM across various models and datasets while preserving in-domain learning. On Llama-3.1-8B finetuned on legal data, TReFT reduces EM by 33.5% more than data interleaving. It also extends to other narrow finetuning settings, reducing off-topic generalization by 54.3% on average, supporting the Piggyback Hypothesis. The work highlights unintended LLM generalization patterns and suggests paths for more constrained finetuning.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究揭示了LLMs在微调过程中导致新兴错配的机制，并提出了一种创新的方法（TReFT）来有效缓解该问题，提升模型在训练域外的对齐性。这不仅对理解语言模型的泛化行为意义重大，也为更安全稳定的模型应用提供了技术保证。
why_it_matters_en: This research uncovers mechanisms behind emergent misalignment caused by finetuning LLMs and introduces an innovative method (TReFT) to effectively mitigate it, improving out-of-domain alignment. It advances understanding of model generalization behaviors and offers a technical approach towards safer and more controlled model deployment.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: The Piggyback Hypothesis of Generalization: Explaining and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
